summary: Matched a batch of fighter, library collision, and map collision routines, with the decomp.dev report showing +6580 matched bytes, +0.17% GALE01 matched code, 17 newly matched functions, and 2 additional unmatched-function improvements. The largest work was in src/melee/lb/lbcollision.c, adding typed GX/HSD collision debug-rendering routines and data definitions; src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c replaced several NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs and adjusted nearby control flow/prototypes; src/melee/mp/mpcoll.c matched mpColl_80043E90.
what_changed: Split the former large lbColl_803B9928 data symbol in config/GALE01/symbols.txt into lbColl_803B9928 plus lbColl_803B9940, lbColl_803B9AE0, lbColl_803B9C80, lbl_803B9DA0, lbl_803B9E20, lbColl_803B9EC0, and lbColl_803B9F20 with explicit sizes.

In lbcollision.c, replaced several extern data placeholders with typed/static data such as GXColor lbColl_804D36D4/lbColl_804D36D8, a GXColor-pointer table lbColl_803B9928, and zero-filled u8 arrays corresponding to the newly split data symbols.

Added lbcollision implementations for GX/HSD collision visualization paths including lbColl_80008DA4, lbColl_80008FC8, lbColl_800096B4, lbColl_8000A10C, and lbColl_8000A1A8, using baselib cobj/state/tev headers and GXColor-typed parameters.
Updated lbcollision.h prototypes from UNK_PARAMS or raw u8*/Vec3-array forms to typed signatures, including GXColor* arguments and struct lbColl_8000A10C_arg0_t*.
Added struct lbColl_8000A10C_arg0_t in lb/types.h and changed it_266F_ItemVars.x1C in it/types.h from Vec3 x1C[2] to that struct type.

In ftcoll.c, replaced several NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs with matched logic: ftColl_80078384, ftColl_800784B4, ftColl_80078710, ftColl_8007AD18, ftColl_8007AE80, ftColl_8007AF60, ftColl_8007AFF8, ftColl_8007B064, and ftColl_8007B6A0.
Adjusted ftcoll prototypes to concrete Fighter*, Fighter_GObj*, HitCapsule*, and UNK_T parameter types for ftColl_800784B4, ftColl_8007861C, and ftColl_80078710.
Changed ftColl_803C0C40 and ftColl_803C0C4C from IntVec3 globals to int arrays, matching the observed data usage as SFX ID tables.
Added small wrapper functions named ftColl_80076808_dontinline and ftColl_8007699C_dontinline and used them at collision call sites that needed different codegen.

Corrected or reshaped ftcoll control flow and constants, including facing_dir assignment branches, ftGrabDist absolute-distance logic, boolean condition spelling, and an OSReport string for illegal fighter hit-status parts.
Extended Fighter struct typing around x1670 and damage-log-related fields: x1670 now exposes x14 and x18, x1870 is a DmgLogEntry pointer, and x187c is float.
Matched mpColl_80043E90 in mpcoll.c and changed its header return type from s32 to bool.
smart_moves: Split a monolithic data symbol into multiple correctly sized symbols before defining arrays in C, which made the lb collision data layout explicit and reusable for later matches.
Introduced GXColor-typed prototypes instead of raw byte pointers for collision drawing functions, improving both type safety and match readability.
Used static data definitions and color tables in lbcollision.c rather than leaving extern placeholders, giving the compiler the correct storage/class layout for matching.
Added narrow struct typing for lbColl_8000A10C_arg0_t and propagated it into item vars, avoiding misleading Vec3[2] modeling for data that includes scalar radius/metadata plus vectors.
Used small call-through wrappers with dontinline-style names where direct calls changed codegen in nearby ftcoll functions.
Kept unknown field names for poorly understood Fighter members while still improving concrete types where evidence was clear, such as DmgLogEntry* and float.
Changed mpColl_80043E90 to bool in both source and header, aligning its API with boolean success/failure usage.
decomp_lessons: When a .data symbol actually spans several independent arrays, split the symbol table entry first; matching C definitions then become much cleaner and less dependent on artificial padding.
Collision debug-rendering functions may require precise GX/HSD state setup calls and typed GXColor globals to match, not just geometric math.
Do not model mixed scalar/vector structs as Vec3 arrays just because the total size is similar; introduce a named placeholder struct when access patterns show scalar fields and vector fields.
SFX lookup tables may match as plain int arrays rather than small vector structs even when the data has three elements.
For matching-sensitive call sites, an explicit wrapper can preserve desired call boundaries when compiler inlining or direct-call codegen differs.
Boolean return types should be reflected in headers once control flow shows true/false semantics; this can help downstream callers match.
Small expression changes such as `!func()` vs `func() == false`, branch order, and explicit fallthrough can be significant in already-near-matching collision loops.
naming_conventions: Address-based names were retained for unknown functions and data: lbColl_80008DA4, lbColl_80008FC8, lbColl_800096B4, ftColl_8007AD18, mpColl_80043E90, lbColl_803B9940.
New placeholder struct name follows the project pattern of tying an unknown argument type to the consuming function: struct lbColl_8000A10C_arg0_t.
Unknown Fighter fields continue to use offset-style names such as x1670, x1870, x187c, x2219_b3, and x2221_b0 until semantics are known.
Helper getUnkVal is named as explicitly uncertain rather than assigning unsupported semantics.
Wrapper names ftColl_80076808_dontinline and ftColl_8007699C_dontinline document their matching purpose directly.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used static inline call-through wrappers to affect call/inlining behavior at ftColl_80078A2C and ftColl_80078C70 call sites.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in ftColl_80078710 to satisfy stack layout/codegen requirements.
Changed data declarations from IntVec3 to int[] to match generated data accesses.
Rewrote conditions into compiler-friendly boolean forms and adjusted branch polarity/order in ftcoll collision loops.
Used explicit GXColor globals and pointer arguments to make lbcollision rendering routines compile to the expected loads/calls.
Inserted fallthrough-style switch logic in ftColl_8007AD18 for HitCapsule_Unk2 to HitCapsule_Max handling.
Adjusted function signatures in headers and source together so caller/callee ABI and return-value handling matched, notably for ftColl_800784B4, ftColl_8007861C, ftColl_80078710, and mpColl_80043E90.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev reported GALE01 matched code at 40.58%, an improvement of +0.17% and +6580 bytes.

decomp.dev listed 17 new matches: lbColl_800096B4, lbColl_80008FC8, lbColl_80008DA4, ftColl_8007AD18, ftColl_80078384, mpColl_80043E90, lbColl_8000A10C, lbColl_8000A1A8, ftColl_800784B4, ftColl_8007AF60, ftColl_8007AFF8, ftColl_8007AE80, ftColl_8007B064, ftColl_8007B6A0, ftColl_80078A2C, ftColl_800788D4, and ftColl_80078710.
decomp.dev reported improvements in lbColl_8000AB2C to 99.86% and ftColl_80078C70 to 97.63%.
decomp.dev reported one regression: ftColl_8007861C decreased from 2.30% to 0.00%, apparently due to changing it from a stub body to an external-style declaration/signature in the slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or type lbColl_8000A10C_arg0_t fields x0/x4/x8/x14.
Search for follow-up work on ftColl_8007861C after the reported decomp.dev regression.
Search for later cleanup of the temporary struct unk around lbColl_803B9928.
Look for PRs that further split or name lbColl_803B9940 through lbColl_803B9F20 data regions.
Find later matches using ftColl_80076808_dontinline or ftColl_8007699C_dontinline as a codegen tactic.
